title: Error: Cannot decrypt message
author: NannoSilver
body: Hello, I am currently running KeePassNatMsg 2.0.17.0 with KeePass 2.61 (x64) on Windows, together with the KeePassXC-Browser 1.10.1 extension (released April 9, 2026) on Firefox 149.0.2. Since a few days ago, the automatic login and password filling has stopped working in Firefox. The KeePassXC-Browser extension now displays the following error: `Cannot decrypt message` I’ve attached a screenshot of the error for reference: It seems the KeePassNatMsg bridge may not be functioning correctly, though I’m not entirely certain—there’s a chance the issue lies elsewhere. Thank you very much for your continued work in maintaining this plugin—it’s greatly appreciated!

title: KeePassXC-Browser cannot connect to KeePass2
author: UltramarineSky
body:
Computer system: Windows  
Browser: Edge Canary  
KeePass version: 2.57.1  

After installing the plugin and downloading **keepassnatmsg-proxy.exe**, the **KeePassXC-Browser** plugin in Edge browser fails to connect to KeePass. Even after restarting KeePass and the computer, it keeps showing "Unable to connect" or "Key exchange was not successful."  

I have tried:  
1. Checking the registry paths `HKCU\SOFTWARE\Google\Chrome\NativeMessagingHosts\org.keepassxc.keepassxc_browser` and the Edge browser's registry. The file paths in the values are correct.  
2. Installing **KeePassXC-Browser** in Chrome browser, which also shows a connection failure.  

Only after reinstalling KeePass can the **KeePassXC-Browser** plugin successfully connect to KeePass.  

Is there any way to resolve this? Or where can I check the running logs?

title: Questions about URL matching mechanism
author: huajideshutiao
body: Is it possible to match aaa.net and aaa.com through aaa? How should I implement this behavior? Should I fill in aaa or regular expression in the URL or custom string?

title: keepassxc-browser:1.9.1 broke from a undefined "group" property
author: wadabum
body:
Please have a look at https://github.com/keepassxreboot/keepassxc-browser/pull/2262 handling https://github.com/keepassxreboot/keepassxc-browser/issues/2260

[Since they seem to have moved the fix to some undated-feature-release](https://github.com/keepassxreboot/keepassxc-browser/pull/2262#issuecomment-2208948091), maybe shimming it on the plugins end is worth a quick fix?
 (using "folder names" is likely the most "group-ish" - but a empty string will likely do just fine too and be quick to add)

And feel free to toss/ignore/close this if keepassxc-browser:1.9.2 has been released of course :)

title: Insatllation via AUR not working (/not showing)
author: lobneroO
body:
I have tried to install KeePass-NatMsg via AUR. The installation itself didn't show errors, but KeePassXC is not showing any entry for it in Tools.

The AUR installs the plugin to /usr/share/keepass/plugin rather than /usr/share/keepass/**P**lugin, but even after manually copying the file it doesn't work. Also, I'm not sure about the unzip step - do I still need to do it when installing through AUR? I tried, but it did not do anything (/work).

I have mono installed (version 6.12), I have restarted KeePassXC. Tools does not show the menu option. I'm on Arch Linux.

title: Ask questions about URL matching rules
author: diaodeng
body:
![image](https://github.com/smorks/keepassnatmsg/assets/108243171/978c5d92-b5b7-4151-bb1d-4e666f54ae07)
Record A sets the field starting with URL and the field starting with KPH: to www.test2.com
Record A default URL is www.test1.com
Record B's default URL is www.test2.com
![image](https://github.com/smorks/keepassnatmsg/assets/108243171/59be17e5-19ef-4a22-b26c-3dc1c6b3e7fb)



Access address: www.test2.com
My understanding is that if I only activate 13 or only activate 14, it should be able to match records A and B, but in reality, no matter how I set it, it can only match record B.
Is it my understanding problem?

Do not want to achieve the above matching logic by copying A.

title: Possible not download KeepassNatMsg-proxy.exe into appdata folder?
author: dust2k
body: I found the plugin is working at home computer perfectly, but when I try at office, because the AD group policy blocked all exe files run in the C:\Users\xxx\AppData\ folder, so the keepassnatmsg-proxy.exe which is in C:\Users\xxx\AppData\Local\KeePassNatMsg can not be launched properly, so is it possible to download the exe file into keepass plugins folder instead of C:\Users\xxx\AppData\ folder? thanks.

title: Change the URL with version information used by the update check to github.com

title: Saving Keys not made database unsaved

title: New Maintainer?
author: smorks
body:
as mentioned here: https://github.com/smorks/keepassnatmsg/pull/102#issuecomment-1343031223

i don't actually use this plugin anymore, therefore i don't have the drive or time to maintain this project. i plan on merging in the current two active pull requests (#101, #102), and creating a new release.

beyond that, does anybody want to take over at the maintainer for this project? i can help with any questions about the current codebase, etc.

thanks!

title: Keepass 2 crashes with keepassnatmsg + KeePassXC-browser update
author: Gobo42
body:
I just started using KeePass 2 with keepassnatmsg and KeePassXC-Browser, so it's all the latest versions. I am finding that when I update a password and KeePassXC-Browser tries to create a new password or update an existing one, the first works, but all subsequent pushes into the database, KeePass crashes and looses all the new passwords I made.

I'm not even sure where to pull debug logs or how to generate debug logs to figure out whats going on. Can someone help? Is this replicable or is there a way I can pull the equivalent of a support package?

Thanks.

title: Support for KeePassOTP
author: Driek123
body:
Hi, sorry I submitted the issue when I pressed enter before filling the description. 

I know the plugin already supports KeeOTP & KeeTrayOTP for TOTP generation, but there isn't support for this one: https://github.com/Rookiestyle/KeePassOTP

Would love if it can be added as well, it uses a different format in the otp field & the placeholder used is {KPOTP}.
